commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the shared session-entry merge helper to rewrite recognized generated sessionFile paths when sessionId changes and adds regression tests for generated, topic, and custom transcript paths.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +54. Total +68 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main's shared merge helper carries existing sessionFile through a sessionId change, and the linked issue discussion includes a deployed stale generated path shape that matches this code path.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action or maintainer judgment is needed for real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair automatically.

target.title: fix: rewrite generated session files on rollover
target.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites generated `sessionFile` paths when a session-store merge changes `sessionId`.
- Prevents stale generated transcript paths like `old-session.jsonl` or `old-session-topic-456.jsonl` from being carried onto a new session id.
- Preserves custom/non-generated `sessionFile` paths when OpenClaw cannot safely derive a replacement.

Fixes #65564.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Session-store rollover can preserve a stale generated `sessionFile` while changing `sessionId`, causing a new logical session id to keep appending to an old transcript file.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node v22.20.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Added regression coverage for generated `old-id.jsonl` rollover, generated `old-id-topic-456.jsonl` rollover, and custom transcript path preservation.

Observed result after fix: All listed verification commands exited 0.

What was not tested: Full repository test suite, live heartbeat runtime, and packaged npm install/upgrade path.
